nameMeanings.name: Kastel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: topographic name for someone living near a castle or fort, from Kastell ‘fort, citadel’ (from Latin castellum), or a habitational name from any of numerous places in Germany so named (more usually Kastell).
Dutch (Van Kastel): same as 1 above, or a variant of Van Gastel, a habitational name for someone from Gastel in North Brabant.

variants.name: Kastelic
meanings: Slovenian: variant of Kostevc, a habitational name denoting someone from the Kostelsko region in Lower Carniola, named after the castle of Kostel. The surname Kastelic may in some cases also be a topographic name for someone who lived in or near a castle or a fort, kastel. Compare Kostelec .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Kastel was born in 1255, in Kastel, Fröhnd, Bezirksamt Schönau, Baden, Germany. She married Sir William Kastel   -AKA Castell. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.
